title: Per-topic write locks are required because SOC feed indices are monotonic and write-once
content:
## Claim

Feed writes must be **serialized per topic** because SOC feed indices are monotonic and write-once — concurrent writes to the same topic race on the next index, and Bee rejects duplicates.

## Evidence

- Bee processes feed writes sequentially per topic (SOC index is monotonic)
- Each index slot can only be written once; two parallel writers both targeting index N produces a conflict
- The adapter's **per-topic `feedWriteLocks`** map guarantees that writes to the same topic are serialized
- Cross-topic parallelism is still fine: the throttled flush allows up to 5 concurrent writes across different topics

## Why it matters

Dropping the per-topic lock would not just slow things down — it would produce silent index collisions where only one of the concurrent updates survives. This lock is correctness-critical, not a performance optimization. The 3-second debounce already batches rapid edits on the same topic into a single write, minimizing contention on the lock.
description: Each feed index can only be written once; concurrent writes to the same feed race on the index. The adapter's feedWriteLocks map enforces serialization per topic.
